texto: Was it possible to love a man who made you feel ridiculous? Of course [.....], love was complicated, that was all. Or was love simple, and marriage was complicated? In seventeen years of marriage David had often left her feeling frustrated, and furious, and disgusted, yes - but he had also made her feel beautiful, and protected, and loved. And oh, what she would give to feel loved right now.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Laura Brodie que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Was it possible to love a man who made you feel ridiculous? Of course [.....], love was complicat…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, death, love, com foco em casamento e luto.

texto: Looking at the children, she knew that any hope for a new life would have to grow within her. She glanced at Nate, wondering if he felt as she did, that children were the only consolation in this world, the only recompense for so much suffering.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Laura Brodie sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Looking at the children, she knew that any hope for a new life would have to grow within her. She…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, children, pregnancy, útil em momentos de pregnancy.
